name: Ironhide Brute

lore: Ironhide Brutes roam the broken badlands and deep forests of the Prime. Hunters who survive an encounter describe the sound of cracking earth beneath their charge — and the silence after.
feature_effect: Charge up to your full movement speed in a straight line. Each creature in its path must succeed on a Physical check or be knocked Prone and take [[1+PL]] Physical damage.

name: Gloomwing Siren
description: A feathered creature with iridescent wings and a haunting call. It lures travelers off paths and into hazardous terrain.
lore: Gloomwings nest in old-growth canopies and sing to confuse wayfarers. Fey scholars believe their call is an evolved mimicry of distress signals from wounded prey.
feature_effect: One creature within short range that can hear the Gloomwing must succeed on a Spirit check or be Charmed until the end of their next turn. While Charmed, they must move toward the Gloomwing on their turn.
feature_duration: Until end of their next turn

name: Ashveil Rot
description: A shambling mass of decayed matter held together by necrotic energy. It spreads a choking ash-cloud wherever it walks.
lore: Ashveils are born from mass graves left untended during Eclipse bleeds. They don't hunger for flesh — they spread rot as a reflex, a kind of contagion given legs.
feature_effect: The Ashveil releases a burst of choking ash in a short radius. Creatures in range must succeed on a Survival check or gain the Coughing condition until the end of their next turn.
feature_duration: Until end of their next turn

effects.effect: Choose a target within short range. They must succeed on a Survival check or become Sickened. While Sickened, the target has the Coughing, Confused, and Exhausted conditions. Each successful Survival check removes one condition of their choice. The sickness remains until all the conditions are cleared

Choose a reach range area within short range. Each creature in the area makes a Survival check. On a failure, the creature becomes Plagued and immediately gains the Coughing, Confused, Exhausted, Blind, and Stunned conditions. On each of their turns, a Plagued creature may make a Survival check, removing one condition of their choice on a success. Plagued ends only when all five conditions have been cleared. While any conditions remain, if a Plagued creature moves within reach range of any living creature, that creature must make a Survival check or become Plagued under the same rules.

name: Shadow Beast
description: Shadow Beasts are the remnants of souls that failed to move on, spirits that lingered too long in the half-light of purgatory until the darkness reshaped them. Time and isolation erode what they once were, twisting memory into hunger and identity into absence. They are not driven by malice so much as entropy and loneliness, drawn to the living as proof that something still exists beyond the dark. In the Eclipse and other light-starved places, Shadow Beasts drift like stains in the air, barely defined until acknowledged. To notice one is to invite it closer. Their most unsettling trait is perceptual reciprocity
lore: Shadow Beasts can only perceive creatures that perceive them. Averted eyes and deliberate ignorance offer a strange kind of protection, while fear and curiosity act as beacons. Many victims swear the creature was not there until the moment they realized it was watching them. Scholars argue whether this is a curse of awareness or a survival mechanism, a final echo of the soul’s need to be recognized before it fully dissolves.
feature_effect: You can only sense creatures that perceive you in return. Creatures outside line of sight or in full cover are Invisible to you. Any creature that observes you immediately reveals their location to you.

features.name: In Sights
features.effect: Double damage on targets that can see you

features.name: Hidden (Shadows)
features.effect: If you remain motionless in darkness, creatures cannot distinguish you from the environment without succeeding on an Observation check.

name: Light Reaver
description: (Squirrel Moth): These creatures are a warning sign of light. they start as dark furred squirrel like creatures with soft mothlike wings that allow them to glide short distance. they seek high places to find sources of light. When they find the light they turn their back to it allowing the light to be absorbed though their wings.
feature_effect: You instinctively target creatures carrying light sources. Gain Advantage on attacks against illuminated creatures.

features.name: Wingflare
features.effect: When damaged by a melee attack, release a burst of stored light. The attacker takes [[PL]] Solar damage.

name: Lich
feature_effect:
You possess a hidden and protected Soul Jar that anchors your existence. You may travel any distance from the Soul Jar without penalty. When you die, your body reforms beside the Soul Jar over 3 days.
If the Soul Jar is destroyed, you permanently die unless a new Soul Jar is created. Creating a new Soul Jar requires harvested souls equal to your PL.

effects.effect: Transport yourself or a willing creature you touch to an unoccupied point within medium range that you can see.
Transport up to 3 willing creatures within reach to an unoccupied point within medium range that you can see.
Transport up to 9 willing creatures within short range to an unoccupied space you are aware of or can see within Long Range. On a failure, you may end up anywhere on any plane.
Transport a long-range area to an unoccupied space you are aware of on this plane. On a failure, the area is teleported to the Eclipse or the Ordealis

Choose the corpse or bones of a Small or larger creature. Raise [[1d4]] PL 1 undead parts such as a hand, jaw, or spine. They act on your command and remain for 1 minute. They are mindless but obedient, and collapse when the spell ends. Requires a physical corpse.
Reanimate a single corpse as a PL 4 undead: Skeleton, Zombie, or Ghoul. It follows your commands and remains for the duration or until destroyed. Requires a mostly intact corpse of appropriate size.

Choose a fresh corpse within range. You resurrect the slain creature for 1 hour, retaining its original form and features. It returns at its original PL or up to PL 8, whichever is lower. The creature is mindless and follows your commands. When the duration ends or it is slain again, it crumbles into inert remains and cannot be revived by any spell again.

Remotely manipulate a small object (open latch, pick up cup, tilt a lever) within short range.

Choose a target within short range. They must succeed on a Survival check or become Sickened. While Sickened, the target has the Coughing, Confused, and Exhausted conditions. Each successful Survival check removes one condition of their choice. The sickness remains until all the conditions are cleared

Choose a reach range area within short range. Each creature in the area makes a Survival check. On a failure, the creature becomes Plagued and immediately gains the Coughing, Confused, Exhausted, Blind, and Stunned conditions. On each of their turns, a Plagued creature may make a Survival check, removing one condition of their choice on a success. Plagued ends only when all five conditions have been cleared. While any conditions remain, if a Plagued creature moves within reach range of any living creature, that creature must make a Survival check or become Plagued under the same rules.

A wave of warped light washes across the area, causing creatures of your choice to fade from sight like passing reflections. Targets gain the Invisible condition for 1 hour.

features.name: Hoard

You know when something is touched, moved, stolen, buried beneath rubble, or left to gather dust and webs in the dark. Distance does not dull this connection. Your hoard is an extension of yourself.

name: Sandstorm
description: The horizon doesn't just vanish; it becomes a memory as the sand washes the world away. The storm informs you of your doom, a wall of grinding rust that ensures this is the last thing you will ever see. Within this swirling void, there is no escape and no flight; the sky is closed. You can only pray your shelter is mana-reinforced, or the wind will tear the structure to shreds in a heartbeat. A creature caught in this gale is no threat to the storm. You are a mere ant beneath the wheels of a truck, waiting to be ground into the silica.
motivation: Move with the winds of the desert

features.name: Sand Veil

The target must make a Survival check or become Pinned and Constrained until the end of their next turn. You can use your Off-Action to force them to take a movement or non-magical action of your choice. Creatures with no blood are immune.

name: Glasswraith
description: The Glasswraith is a jagged, floating constellation of amber and violet fulgurite. It has no solid torso; instead, four to six primary shards of sand-fused glass orbit a central, humming core of raw mana. Arcs of violet lightning constantly leap between these fragments, acting as "limbs" that stretch and snap with a sound like a cracking whip. In the center of its largest shard, a flickering, eye-like light pulses in rhythm with the desert’s ambient static. It doesn't walk, but glides inches above the dunes, leaving a glass-melted trail in the sand. When it moves quickly, the shards tilt and catch the light, making the creature appear to vanish into the heat haze before reappearing feet away.
lore:
Known to nomads as the "Vengeance of the Storm," Glasswraiths are the physical scars of failed magic. They are born when a high-tier lightning spell is catastrophically miscast or countered during a desert manastorm, grounding the spell's "intent" into the silica. Lacking a soul but possessing a directive, they hunt for conductors to complete the circuit they were originally meant to close. They are drawn to the bio-electric hum of living hearts and the concentrated mana of spellcasters.

While they are aggressive and relentless hunters, they are fundamentally preservationists of their own fragile forms. Because they are held together by a precise magnetic frequency, structural instability is a death sentence. When you are significantly injured, the electrical tethers between your shards begin to fray and sputter; rather than risk total shattering, you will immediately collapse your form into a single, inert shard and flee through the sand or air to find a manastorm to recharge.
